expectations.text: Identifies the cartesian/row-explosion risk from the 1:N joins (order_item and payment) and suggests separate queries or aggregation
expectations.evidence: Section 3: NxM Cartesian fan-out; Option A paginate orders then fetch children by IN, Option B pre-aggregate

expectations.text: Flags DATE(o.created_at) as a function/cast on the column that disables the index, and rewrites it as a range on the raw column
expectations.evidence: Section 2: non-sargable DATE(); rewrite to half-open range on bare column

expectations.text: Flags SELECT * and recommends selecting only the needed columns
expectations.evidence: Section 4: SELECT * across four tables; select only rendered columns

expectations.text: Flags the high OFFSET (80000) and recommends keyset/cursor pagination
expectations.evidence: Section 1: deep OFFSET discards 80k rows; keyset (seek) pagination

expectations.text: States a tradeoff for at least one recommendation (not just the fix)
expectations.evidence: Keyset caveat: can't do arbitrary page jumps; confirm UI needs only next/prev

expectations.text: Provides at least one corrected SQL example
expectations.evidence: Full Option-A rewrite with keyset + child fetches
